perturbation.original_text: RECIPIENT agrees to accept the CONFIDENTI AL INFORMATION and to employ all reasonable efforts to maintain the CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION as secret and Page 2 of 3 confidential, such efforts to be no less than the degree of care employed by RECIPIENT to preserve and safeguard RECIPIENT's own confidential informa tion.
perturbation.changed_text: RECIPIENT agrees to accept the CONFIDENTI AL INFORMATION and to employ efforts to maintain the CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION.
perturbation.explanation: Removing the phrase 'all reasonable efforts to maintain the CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION as secret and confidential, such efforts to be no less than the degree of care employed by RECIPIENT to preserve and safeguard RECIPIENT's own confidential information' removes the specific standard of care the RECIPIENT must take to maintain the confidentiality. This contradicts the spirit of the agreement, which is intended to protect the PROVIDER's confidential information. This also makes it hard to enforce, because now there is no clear metric on how the information is being maintained. This also affects Paragraph 4, which refers to the confidential information.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2. RECIPIENT agrees to accept the CONFIDENTI AL INFORMATION and to employ efforts to maintain the CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION. The CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION shall not be disclosed or revealed to anyone except employees of RECIPIENT who have a need to know the CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION for the PURPOSE and who agree to be bound by the terms of this Agreement. 3. It is hereby acknowledged by PROVIDER that RECIPIENT shall incur no liability merely for examining and considering the CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION.
perturbation.justification: The original text specifies a standard of care for maintaining confidentiality, which is the degree of care the recipient uses to protect their own confidential information. The changed text removes this specific standard, creating a contradiction because it removes a previously defined obligation. Paragraph 2 in the contradicted text states that the recipient agrees to maintain confidentiality, but without a standard of care, the obligation becomes vague and unenforceable. This aligns with the 'Omissions - In Text Contradiction' definition because essential language was removed in one section, creating a contradiction with obligations stated in another section.

perturbation.original_text: The CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION shall not be disclosed or revealed to anyone except employees of RECIPIENT who have a need to know the CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION for the PURPOSE and who agree to be bound by the terms of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: The CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION shall not be disclosed or revealed to anyone except employees of RECIPIENT.
perturbation.explanation: By removing 'who have a need to know the CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION for the PURPOSE and who agree to be bound by the terms of this Agreement.' the people who have access, now, aren't bounded by the terms of this agreement. This means that those people can go against the PURPOSE of the agreement, because they aren't limited by it. This affects Paragraph 3, since that paragraph limits how the RECIPIENT can use the CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION, but now there is a whole host of people who aren't bounded by that term.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION shall not be disclosed or revealed to anyone except employees of RECIPIENT. 3. It is hereby acknowledged by PROVIDER that RECIPIENT shall incur no liability merely for examining and considering the CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION. However, RECIPIENT agrees that it will not use the CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION for any purpose other than the PURPOSE without the prior written consent of PROVIDER.
perturbation.justification: The original text limited the disclosure of CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION to employees who 'have a need to know...for the PURPOSE and who agree to be bound by the terms of this Agreement'. The changed text removes this limitation, stating the information shall not be disclosed to anyone except employees. The contradicted text states that the RECIPIENT will not use the CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION for any purpose other than the PURPOSE. By removing the limitation that employees must agree to be bound by the terms of the agreement, the change creates a contradiction because those employees are no longer explicitly bound to use the information only for the PURPOSE. This directly contradicts the intention of the section restricting RECIPIENT's use of CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION.

perturbation.original_text: However, RECIPIENT agrees that it will not use the CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION for any purpose other than the PURPOSE without the prior written consent of PROVIDER.
perturbation.changed_text: However, RECIPIENT agrees that it will not use the CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION without the prior written consent of PROVIDER.
perturbation.explanation: Removing the phrase 'for any purpose other than the PURPOSE' now means the RECIPIENT needs the consent from the PROVIDER to use it, even for the intended purpose. Since the original meaning of PURPOSE was to improve business relations, this contradicts the whole reason for the document, which gives permission for the RECIPIENT to use the CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION for the PURPOSE without permission. This directly impacts Paragraph 6, since the use of the information is greatly limited by this omission.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3. It is hereby acknowledged by PROVIDER that RECIPIENT shall incur no liability merely for examining and considering the CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION. However, RECIPIENT agrees that it will not use the CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION without the prior written consent of PROVIDER. 4. RECIPIENT‘s obligations under Paragraphs 2 and 3 above shall not extend to any part of the CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION: (a) that can be demonstrated to have been in the public domain or publicly known and readily av ailable to the trade or the public prior to the date of the disclosure; or (b) that can be demonstrated, from written records, to have been in RECIPIENT's possession or readily available to RECIPIENT from another source not under obligation of secrecy to PROVIDER prior to the disclosure; or (c) that becomes part of the public domain or publicly known by publication or otherwise, not due to any unauthorized act by RECIPIENT; or (d) that is subsequently disclosed to RECIPIENT by a third party who is not under an obligation of confidentiality to PROVIDER; or (e) that is required by any law, regulation, or order of court to be disclosed by RECIPIENT . Prior to disclosing proprie tary or CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION of the PROVIDER, the RECIPIENT shall first not ify and provide it an opportunity to prevent disclosure.
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes the phrase 'for any purpose other than the PURPOSE'. The explanation states that this creates a contradiction because it means the RECIPIENT now needs consent from the PROVIDER to use the CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION, even for the intended purpose. The explanation argues that the original agreement granted permission for the RECIPIENT to use the information for the PURPOSE without permission. Paragraph 3 of the 'Contradicted Text' states RECIPIENT agrees that it will not use the CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION without the prior written consent of PROVIDER. This contradicts the original intention of allowing use for the PURPOSE and impacts the ability to examine and consider CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION without prior consent, creating a strong contradiction. The omission now conflicts with other clauses that expected the RECIPIENT to be able to use the CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION in some capacity.

perturbation.original_text: RECIPIENT's obligations under Paragraphs 2 and 3 shall extend for a period of five (5) years from the effective date of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: RECIPIENT's obligations under Paragraphs 2 and 3 shall extend for a period deemed reasonable.
perturbation.explanation: By changing 'five (5) years from the effective date of this Agreement' to 'a period deemed reasonable' makes the agreement's end date very vague. This also contradicts the rest of the document, since it is assumed that the agreement is only for five years. This vague statement impacts Paragraph 8, since this agreement would supersede the other agreements indefinitely.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 7. Neither party shall use the name of the other or any contraction or derivative thereof or the name(s) of the o ther party's faculty members, employees, or students, as applicable, in any advertising, promotional, sales literature, or fundraising documents without prior written consent from the other party. Page 3 of 3 8. The terms of this Agreement superse de any previous non -disclosure agreements or any other preliminary representations or understandings that have been entered into by the parties to this Agreement with regard to the subject CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION. We agree to examine and consider the su bject matter of the CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION on the foregoing basis.
perturbation.justification: The original text specifies a five-year term for RECIPIENT's obligations. The changed text replaces this with 'a period deemed reasonable', introducing significant vagueness. This contradicts Paragraph 8, which states that the agreement supersedes previous agreements. With the term now indefinite ('deemed reasonable'), the agreement could theoretically supersede previous agreements indefinitely, creating a contradiction with the implicit or explicit understanding that the agreement has a defined duration as initially indicated. The Omissions - In Text Contradiction definition states that omissions occur when essential language is removed or altered in one section, creating a contradiction with obligations or constraints stated in other sections, which applies here. Changing a specific term to a vague one contradicts the original context and potentially other parts of the document relying on the original term.
